scope: Next larger board-impact batch, focusing on unprojected players with strong production anchors, high-major role evidence, or clear returning-star upside.
method: Skip transfer rows without a confirmed current team until destination data can be resolved.
Prioritize proven returners/transfers with enough role evidence to affect the leaderboard.
Use projected-lineup, school roster, official bio, award, usage, and transfer-context evidence to score projected starter and role.
Keep high-major bench or crowded-roster transfers below proven starters even when historical rating is strong.
Leave every row pending admin approval so the larger review queue can apply the batch in one pass.

formulas.projectedBbprWithHistory: historicalRating/proven production blended with projection score, then calibrated by player type

teamContextRubric.projectedStarter: Yes requires projected-lineup, start-history, or strong returning hierarchy; Uncertain means strong minutes without a locked starting signal; No means bench/developmental role.
teamContextRubric.projectedRole: 90+ for clear starters/top options, 82-89 for likely starters or sixth-man/major rotation, 72-81 for useful but crowded roles.
teamContextRubric.opportunityChange: Rewards teams with open usage/minutes or returning focal points; penalizes crowded high-major rotations.
teamContextRubric.offensiveBurden: Primary scorers/creators get 86+; lower-usage bigs/connective pieces stay lower.
teamContextRubric.roleEvidence: Every row includes why starter credit was assigned or capped.

players.researchSummary: Hines is young, productive, and physical enough to matter for UConn, but the Huskies' frontcourt is crowded and his path looks more like major rotation/possible starter than automatic high-usage starter.

teamContext.starterEvidence: UConn added Hines after he started 31 games as a freshman, but roster context includes multiple older bigs and forwards.
teamContext.roleCap: High-major frontcourt rotation with starter upside, not a clear offensive focal point.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Tarris Reed Jr., Eric Reibe, Nils Machowski, and other UConn pieces cap minutes certainty.

evidence.notes: Roster bio lists Hines after a freshman season with 31 starts and 8.4 PPG at Seton Hall.

players.researchSummary: Buchanan's scoring transfer profile and West Virginia's need for frontcourt offense give him a clear role bump. His final score stays in a good-starter range because the NBA/upside signal is modest.

teamContext.starterEvidence: West Virginia signed Buchanan after double-figure scoring and transfer-market attention, creating a plausible starting forward path.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely starting or closing forward with scoring value, but not an elite national anchor.
teamContext.positionCompetition: West Virginia added multiple new pieces, so final burden depends on how the transfer class settles.

evidence.notes: Reports Buchanan signing with West Virginia after productive transfer seasons.

players.researchSummary: Leonhardt was a productive creator at Utah Valley, but Nebraska has returning high-end pieces and transfer guard competition. He gets meaningful rotation/secondary-creator credit without a starter lock.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Nebraska signed Leonhardt after strong WAC guard production, but public lineup certainty is not strong.
teamContext.roleCap: Secondary ball handler/rotation guard moving up in competition.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Pryce Sandfort, Braden Frager, and Nebraska's guard additions keep usage shared.

evidence.notes: Nebraska announced Leonhardt after a productive Utah Valley career.

players.researchSummary: Lewis has a strong high-major body of work and USC needed veteran toughness on the wing. He gets starter/closing-lineup credit, but USC's many portal pieces keep him below top-option scorers.

teamContext.starterEvidence: USC announced Lewis as a veteran Big East/Big 12 transfer with immediate defensive and slashing value.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely starting wing or sixth starter, not a primary offensive creator.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Rodney Rice, Eric Reibe, Jadis Jones, and other USC additions distribute touches.

evidence.notes: USC signs Lewis after high-major experience at Arizona and Georgetown.

players.researchSummary: Little should be treated as a clear mid-major lead guard: high-assist role, starter profile, and one of UCSB's best proven pieces. NBA/upside prevents a massive jump, not opportunity.

teamContext.starterEvidence: UCSB roster context lists Little as an experienced returning guard after a high-minute season.
teamContext.roleCap: Lead-guard role at UCSB with strong assist burden.
teamContext.positionCompetition: UCSB has other guards, but Little's experience gives him the cleanest creation case.

evidence.notes: Roster bio documents Little's guard role and prior production.

players.researchSummary: Evans brings former five-star tools and defensive versatility to Villanova, but his college production has not yet matched the pedigree. Starter/role credit is real, while offensive burden is capped.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Villanova added Evans as a major transfer forward, but Tyler Perkins and other additions keep the offense shared.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely starting/closing forward with upside but uneven proven scoring.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Perkins, Crawford, Royal, Fiegen, and Oumiddoch spread frontcourt/wing usage.

evidence.notes: Villanova announced Evans after two seasons at Oregon and former five-star recruiting status.

players.researchSummary: Djobet projects as one of Texas State's best offensive players after star-level Omaha usage. Role is very high, but final BBPR is restrained by lower draft/upside inputs.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Texas State signed him after major scoring production and should give him a featured role.
teamContext.roleCap: Primary or co-primary Sun Belt scorer, modest NBA signal.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Texas State has several transfers, but Djobet's prior usage gives him a clean path.

evidence.notes: Texas State announced Djobet after standout production at Omaha.

players.researchSummary: Sumler's scoring is real, but Pitt's guard room is crowded and the jump from Charleston Southern to ACC play is meaningful. Good rotation scorer, not a locked starter.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Pitt added Sumler after strong scoring, but public roster context does not guarantee a starting spot.
teamContext.roleCap: Microwave/secondary scorer with starter path if efficiency translates.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Pitt's transfer-heavy backcourt and returning wings create role competition.

evidence.notes: Pitt announced Sumler after productive scoring seasons at Charleston Southern.

players.researchSummary: Elmer gives Wisconsin athletic frontcourt depth and MAC production, but his role is not as clear as a returning starter's. Keep him in a rotation-forward tier.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Wisconsin added Elmer after a productive Miami (OH) season, but starter signal is not firm.
teamContext.roleCap: Rotation forward with size/athletic upside.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Wisconsin's returning frontcourt and portal pieces limit immediate role certainty.

evidence.notes: Wisconsin announced Elmer as a transfer forward from Miami (OH).

players.researchSummary: Jones was a productive scorer moving up several levels. USC's backcourt/wing competition keeps him more in the rotation-scoring tier than the projected-starter tier.

teamContext.starterEvidence: USC signed Jones after strong Lindenwood scoring, but no clean projected-starter evidence surfaced.
teamContext.roleCap: Scoring guard/wing with translation risk.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Rodney Rice, KJ Lewis, Eric Reibe, and other USC additions reduce role certainty.

evidence.notes: USC announced Jones after standout production at Lindenwood.

players.researchSummary: L'Etang has a returning-starter frontcourt path and enough size/tools to pop, but Dayton's offense should not run through him every possession. Starter role, moderate offensive burden.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Dayton roster context keeps L'Etang as an important returning big.
teamContext.roleCap: Starting/major-minute center with development upside.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Dayton's guard creation and transfer additions keep scoring burden shared.

evidence.notes: Roster bio lists L'Etang as a returning Dayton frontcourt player.

players.researchSummary: Kharchenkov has strong international pedigree and sophomore development upside, but Arizona's roster is too talented to hand him a major starter score yet.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Arizona lists him as a returning guard/wing with international background; no clear starter evidence.
teamContext.roleCap: Developmental rotation piece with real tools.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Arizona's high-end returning and incoming talent makes minutes competitive.

evidence.notes: Roster bio lists Kharchenkov's Arizona return and international profile.

players.researchSummary: Power should carry a big Ivy League scoring role after transferring down from high-major basketball. The projection rewards role/opportunity more than recent historical anchor.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Penn roster/transfer context gives Power a clear featured-forward path.
teamContext.roleCap: Primary or co-primary Ivy scorer, but recent production has not matched recruiting pedigree.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Penn has other returning pieces, but Power owns the cleanest size/skill scoring upside.

evidence.notes: Roster bio lists Power at Penn after prior high-major stops.

players.researchSummary: Perry has guard skill and prior prospect pedigree, but UCLA's guard/wing competition keeps him from a full starter projection. Treat as a key rotation guard with upside.

teamContext.starterEvidence: UCLA lists Perry as a returning guard, but projected lineup certainty is limited.
teamContext.roleCap: Rotation/possible starter guard in a crowded Big Ten roster.
teamContext.positionCompetition: UCLA's transfer and returning guards distribute ball-handling.

evidence.notes: Roster bio lists Perry as a returning UCLA guard.

players.researchSummary: Stojakovic should be lifted well above his historical anchor because Illinois acquired him to be a featured scorer and NBA-facing wing. This is a role/talent projection more than a pure past-production projection.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Illinois roster and transfer coverage frame Stojakovic as a marquee scoring wing addition/returning listed player.
teamContext.roleCap: Featured wing scorer with NBA tools, though Illinois has other usage-capable guards/bigs.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Tomislav Ivisic, Kylan Boswell, Quentin Coleman, and Lucas Morillo keep usage from being one-man.

evidence.notes: Roster bio lists Stojakovic at Illinois with prior high-major scoring profile.
Frames Stojakovic as a high-impact Illinois scoring addition.

players.researchSummary: Rice has a clean veteran scoring role for USC and should be one of the roster's primary perimeter options. The final score stays outside the elite range because the historical anchor is moderate.

teamContext.starterEvidence: USC roster/coverage points to Rice as a central veteran guard.
teamContext.roleCap: Lead or co-lead scorer, with several transfer scorers around him.
teamContext.positionCompetition: KJ Lewis, Jadis Jones, and USC's young bigs/guards share possessions.

evidence.notes: Roster bio lists Rice as a returning USC guard.

players.researchSummary: Prey has real international/frontcourt tools but the St. John's role is not clean enough yet for major starter credit. He belongs in the upside rotation tier.

teamContext.starterEvidence: St. John's roster lists Prey, but public context does not clearly lock him into a starting job.
teamContext.roleCap: Toolsy big/forward with rotation path.
teamContext.positionCompetition: St. John's roster remains deep and frontcourt minutes are not settled.

evidence.notes: Roster bio lists Prey at St. John's.

players.researchSummary: Page has size, high-major pedigree, and a plausible Providence frontcourt starting path. Scoring burden stays capped because his best role is interior finishing/defense.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Providence added Page as a frontcourt transfer with high-major experience.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely starting/major-minute big, lower offensive burden.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Providence has multiple new frontcourt pieces and guards to distribute usage.

evidence.notes: Providence announced Page as a transfer addition from Northwestern.

players.researchSummary: Thiam's size and rim protection give him a strong Michigan role case, but Michigan also has Elliott Cadeau and other usage options. Value is defense/finishing more than offensive volume.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Michigan added Thiam as a center transfer with major size and shot-blocking appeal.
teamContext.roleCap: Starting/major-minute center with low-to-moderate offensive burden.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Michigan's guard and wing additions should lead the offense.

evidence.notes: Michigan announced Thiam as a transfer center from Cincinnati.

players.researchSummary: Ivisic is a proven starting-caliber Big Ten big with production, size, and NBA-ish skill. He should be well above generic returning-player territory even if Stojakovic has more scoring upside.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Illinois roster and prior role make Ivisic a central returning big.
teamContext.roleCap: Starting center/forward with scoring, rebounding, and spacing value.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Stojakovic and perimeter pieces share offensive hierarchy.

evidence.notes: Roster bio lists Ivisic as a returning Illinois frontcourt player.

players.researchSummary: Janowski has the right profile for a big Davidson role: young transfer guard with proven scoring/creation. His score is role-driven rather than NBA-driven.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Davidson announced Janowski as a transfer guard with prior starting-level production.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely lead/secondary creator at Davidson, but modest national upside signal.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Davidson's returning guard mix creates some shared usage, though Janowski's scoring should travel.

evidence.notes: Davidson announced Janowski as a transfer guard from St. Thomas.

players.researchSummary: Dixon provides experienced guard/wing depth for Virginia, but the roster has multiple guards and no clear evidence of a locked starting spot. Keep him in a useful rotation tier.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Virginia added Dixon after UC Irvine production, but projected-lineup signal is uncertain.
teamContext.roleCap: Rotation guard/wing with defensive and connective value.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Virginia's guard transfers and returning pieces create a crowded minutes picture.

evidence.notes: Virginia announced Dixon as a transfer from UC Irvine.

players.researchSummary: Freeman has the strongest Auburn frontcourt role case among this batch's transfers. He gets starter credit, but Auburn's perimeter talent and frontcourt depth keep offensive burden moderate.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Auburn signed Freeman as a major frontcourt transfer from Creighton/Iowa.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely starting big with efficient scoring/rebounding rather than alpha usage.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Kevin Overton, Tahaad Pettiford, and other Auburn pieces share offense.

evidence.notes: Auburn announced Freeman as a transfer frontcourt addition.

players.researchSummary: Hubbard should be treated as an elite proven college guard despite a lower historical anchor in this file. His role, shot volume, awards profile, and national recognition justify a major leaderboard lift.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Mississippi State roster/history establishes Hubbard as the team's lead guard and high-usage scorer.
teamContext.roleCap: Primary scorer and offensive engine, with NBA signal good but not top-lottery level.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Mississippi State will add pieces around him, but Hubbard remains the clear offensive hierarchy leader.

evidence.notes: Roster bio documents Hubbard's lead-guard production and Mississippi State career accolades.
Includes Hubbard among notable returning-player names for 2026-27.

players.researchSummary: Cadeau's creation role at Michigan should be strong, especially as a veteran high-major point guard. His scoring limitations keep him below the most explosive guards, but the role is very real.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Michigan lists Cadeau as a returning guard after prior high-major starting experience.
teamContext.roleCap: Lead guard/playmaker with shared scoring load.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Stojakovic-type wings/frontcourt additions and Michigan's other guards share shots.

evidence.notes: Roster bio lists Cadeau at Michigan after prior UNC production.

players.researchSummary: Fland has the right blend of former five-star pedigree, high-major guard creation, and Florida lead-guard opportunity to jump well above his historical anchor. He should mix near the top returner/transfer guards, not be treated as a generic returner.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Florida roster context places Fland in line for a major guard role.
teamContext.roleCap: High-usage lead guard, though Florida's title-level roster culture spreads offense.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Florida has multiple guards/wings, but Fland's shot creation is the clearest ceiling swing.

evidence.notes: Roster bio lists Fland at Florida with prior high-major guard profile.

players.researchSummary: Henderson is a strong sophomore leap candidate with high recruiting pedigree and an opening for Miami to feature young talent. He gets a real development/role lift, but not enough evidence yet for the elite proven tier.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Miami roster lists Henderson as a returning forward with major prospect pedigree.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely starter/major-minute forward with breakout upside.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Miami's transfer guards and frontcourt additions share usage.

evidence.notes: Roster bio lists Henderson as a returning Miami forward.

players.researchSummary: McKenney should receive a major sophomore/new-star bump because his pedigree and Michigan role give him a higher ceiling than the historical rating implies. The score still respects that he has to prove top-option college efficiency.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Michigan lists McKenney as a returning guard/wing with major recruit profile and projected role opportunity.
teamContext.roleCap: Breakout wing/guard scorer with shared creation.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Elliott Cadeau, Moustapha Thiam, and other Michigan additions keep the hierarchy competitive.

evidence.notes: Roster bio lists McKenney as a returning Michigan guard/wing.
